title: Pentaschistis insularis
taxonomy: Pentaschistis insularis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a monocotyledonous plant, belonging to the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Pentaschistis insularis is further divided into the superorder Lilianae, order Poales, and subfamily Danthonioideae. The species is endemic to a specific region, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics. Further research is needed to provide a more detailed classification of Pentaschistis insularis.

intro: Pentaschistis insularis, also known as the subalpine hair sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
